title: Periodic Table & Periodicity Test - Chapter Test

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: In a Telescope, compared to the eyepiece lens the objective lens has
options: larger focal length and larger aperture
larger focal length and smaller aperture
smaller focal length and larger aperture
smaller focal length and smaller aperture
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

$$\text{Option 1: larger focal length and larger aperture}$$

Step-by-step:

  1. In a telescope, the objective lens is the lens facing the distant object.

  2. Its job is to collect as much light as possible from faraway objects.

  3. So, it must have a large aperture.

Also:

  1. The objective lens forms a real, inverted, and diminished image of the distant object.

  2. For better magnification in a telescope, the objective lens should have a large focal length.

  3. The eyepiece acts like a magnifying glass, so it has a small focal length.

Therefore, compared to the eyepiece, the objective lens has:

  • larger focal length

  • larger aperture

Hence, the answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 1}}$$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following astronomers proved that the Earth and other planets revolve around the Sun?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option A: Copernicus

Nicolaus Copernicus was the astronomer who formulated a model of the universe that placed the Sun rather than the Earth at its center, a concept known as heliocentrism. This model was revolutionary and paved the way for future astronomers to further develop and confirm the idea that the Earth and other planets revolve around the Sun.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: There is a ball of mass 320 g. It has 625 J potential energy when released freely from a height. The speed with which it will hit the ground is
questions.solution:
First convert the mass to kilograms:

m = 320 g = 0.32 kg

All the potential energy turns into kinetic energy at the bottom:

$$\tfrac12\,m\,v^2 = \text{PE} = 625\text{ J}$$

Solving for v:

$$v = \sqrt{\frac{2\cdot625}{0.32}} = \sqrt{3906.25} = 62.5\text{ m/s}$$

So the ball hits the ground at 62.5 m/s (Option 1).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: A rectangle ABCD is kept in front of a concave mirror of focal length f with its corners A and B being, respectively, at distances 2f and 3f from the mirror with AB along the principal axis as shown in the figure. It forms an image A'B'C'D' in front of the mirror. What is the ratio of B'C' to A'D'?
questions.solution:
The Mirror Equation:

When rays emanating from a point meet at another point after reflection or refraction, that point is termed the image of the initial point.

The image is termed real if the rays actually converge at that point.

It is virtual if the rays do not actually meet but appear to diverge from that point when extended backward.

The mirror equation is given by: $\frac{1}{f} = \frac{1}{v} + \frac{1}{u}$

Linear magnification (m) is defined as the ratio of the height of the image ($h'$) to the height of the object ($h$): $m = \frac{h'}{h} = -\frac{v}{u}$

Calculation:

Given rectangle ABCD with AD = BC ---- (1)

Corners A and B are at distances 2f and 3f from the mirror, respectively.

For corner A (u = 2f): $ \frac{1}{f} = \frac{1}{v} + \frac{1}{2f} \implies v = 2f $

Magnification: $ m = \frac{h'}{h} = -\frac{v}{u} = -\frac{2f}{2f} = -1 $ This means the size of the image is equal to the size of the object: $ AD = A'D' ---- (2) $

For corner B (u = 3f): $ \frac{1}{f} = \frac{1}{v} + \frac{1}{3f} \implies v = \frac{3}{2}f $

Magnification: $ m = \frac{h'}{h} = -\frac{v}{u} = -\frac{\frac{3}{2}f}{3f} = -\frac{1}{2} $ This means the size of the image is half the size of the object: $ B'C' = \frac{1}{2}BC ---- (3) $

From equations 1, 2, and 3, the ratio of B'C' to A'D' is: $ \frac{B'C'}{A'D'} = \frac{\frac{1}{2}BC}{AD} = \frac{1}{2} $

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Shown in the figure are two hollow cubes C1 and C2 of negligible mass partially filled (depicted by darkened area) with liquids of densities ρ1 and ρ2, respectively, floating in water (density ρw). The relationship between ρ1, ρ2 and ρw is
options: ρ2 < ρ1 < ρw
ρ1 < ρw < ρ2
questions.solution:
Key Idea:

When something floats, it means it is lighter (has a lower average density) than the liquid it is in.

For floating, the weight of water pushed aside by the object is equal to the weight of the object.

If an object sinks deeper (is more submerged), it means its average density is higher.

For hollow cubes with very small weight, the heaviness (density) of the cube comes only from the liquid that is inside them.

What the Figure Shows:

The figure has two cubes, C1 and C2, each filled with a different liquid. The densities of these liquids are ρ1 and ρ2.

C2 is deeper in the water than C1. This means C2 is heavier (has a greater density) than C1.

Because the cubes themselves do not weigh much, the only thing that affects how deep they float is the density of the liquid inside.

So, the density ρ1 (inside C1) is greater than ρ2 (inside C2).

Both cubes float. This tells us that the density of the liquids inside must be less than the density of water (ρw), or otherwise they would sink.

Putting all this together:

ρ2 < ρ1 < ρw

Answer: ρ2 < ρ1 < ρw

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one among the. following figures correctly represents the ray diagram ? (Consider the lens to be thin)
questions.solution:
CONCEPT:

Ray Diagrams for Lenses

A ray diagram is a drawing that shows how light rays move through a lens.

There are three main rays we usually draw for a thin lens:

First Ray: If a ray comes in parallel to the main (principal) axis, it will bend and pass through the lens's focus on the other side.

Second Ray: If a ray goes through the center of the lens, it travels straight and does not bend.

Third Ray: If a ray goes toward the focus before reaching the lens, it will bend after passing through the lens and move out parallel to the main axis.

EXPLANATION:

For a convex lens (one that bulges out):

- A ray that comes in parallel to the main axis will bend and go through the focus on the other side.

- A ray that travels through the middle of the lens goes straight and does not change direction.

- A ray that heads toward the focus before entering the lens will come out of the lens going parallel to the main axis.

So, the right figure for the ray diagram is Option 3.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following optical phenomena supports that the light is a transverse wave?
questions.solution:
The phenomenon that supports the idea that light is a transverse wave is Option D: Polarization.

To understand why polarization is indicative of light being a transverse wave, let's delve into the concept a bit more:

Light, as an electromagnetic wave, consists of oscillating electric and magnetic fields that are perpendicular to each other and to the direction of wave propagation. In the case of a transverse wave, the oscillations occur in a direction perpendicular to the direction of the wave's advance.

Polarization refers to the orientation of the oscillations of the light wave. When light passes through a polarizing filter, the filter only allows waves oscillating in a particular direction to pass through. This demonstrates that light waves have orientations perpendicular to their direction of travel, which is a key characteristic of transverse waves.

To summarize why the other options are not indicative of light being a transverse wave:

Option A: Refraction: A phenomenon where light changes direction when it passes from one medium to another due to a change in speed. This is related to the wave nature of light but does not specifically indicate that light is transverse.

Option B: Diffraction: The bending of light waves around obstacles and the spreading of light waves when they pass through small openings. While this is also a wave property, it is not specific to light being transverse.

Option C: Interference: This is the phenomenon where two or more light waves superpose to form a resultant wave. It demonstrates the wave nature of light but does not specifically indicate that light is a transverse wave.

Therefore, polarization is the phenomenon that confirms the transverse nature of light waves, as it directly shows that light has specific orientations of vibration perpendicular to the direction of propagation.

The mathematical representation for polarized light can be expressed if one considers the electric field vector $$\vec{E}$$ which oscillates in a specific plane, indicating transverse wave characteristics:

$$\vec{E}(z,t) = E_0 \cos(kz - \omega t + \phi) \hat{e}$$

where:

  • $$E_0$$ is the amplitude of the electric field
  • $$k$$ is the wave number
  • $$\omega$$ is the angular frequency
  • $$\phi$$ is the phase constant
  • $$\hat{e}$$ is the unit vector indicating the direction of polarization

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The rule to determine the direction of a force experienced by a straight current carrying conductor placed in a magnetic field which is perpendicular to it is
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option 2: Fleming’s left-hand rule

Explanation:

When a straight current-carrying conductor is placed in a magnetic field perpendicular to it, it experiences a force.

To find the direction of this force, we use Fleming’s left-hand rule.

Fleming’s left-hand rule:

Stretch the thumb, forefinger, and middle finger of your left hand so that they are mutually perpendicular.

Then:

  • Forefinger shows the direction of magnetic field

  • Middle finger shows the direction of current

  • Thumb shows the direction of force or motion

Why other options are wrong:

  • Right-hand thumb rule: Used to find the direction of magnetic field around a current-carrying conductor.

  • Fleming’s right-hand rule: Used for direction of induced current in electromagnetic induction.

  • Hund’s rule: Related to filling of electrons in atomic orbitals.

So, the answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{Fleming’s left-hand rule}}$$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following about different frictional forces is correct?
questions.solution:
Let's examine and compare the different types of friction: static friction, kinetic friction, and rolling friction.

Static Friction : This is the friction that exists between a stationary object and the surface it's on. It needs to be overcome to start moving the object. It is generally the highest among the three because it involves overcoming the intermolecular forces between the surfaces.

Kinetic Friction : This is the friction that acts between moving surfaces. It is typically lower than static friction because once an object is in motion, less force is required to keep it moving compared to starting the motion.

Rolling Friction : This is the friction that occurs when an object rolls over a surface. Rolling friction is generally much smaller than both static and kinetic friction because the area of contact between the two surfaces is much smaller.

Therefore, the correct order from highest to lowest is generally :

$$\text{Static friction} > \text{Kinetic friction} > \text{Rolling friction}$$

Based on this understanding, the correct choice is :

Option C : Static friction > Kinetic friction > Rolling friction

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following regarding density of water at atmospheric pressure is correct?
options: Density of water at 4°C is 1000 kg/m<sup>3</sup>.
Density of water at 0°C is 1000 kg/m<sup>3</sup>.
Density of water at 0°C is 100 kg/m<sup>3</sup>.
Density of water at 4°C is 10 kg/m<sup>3</sup>.
questions.solution:
Option A is correct.

The density of water at 4°C is approximately 1000 kg/m³. At this temperature, water is at its maximum density. This value is derived from the definition of the kilogram, which was historically based on the mass of 1 liter of water at its maximum density point, or 4°C.

Water exhibits anomalous expansion, meaning that it reaches its maximum density not at the freezing point (0°C) but slightly above it at 4°C. Below 4°C, water begins to expand as it approaches the freezing point. This is one reason why ice floats on water—because its density is less than that of liquid water.

The density of water decreases as the temperature increases beyond 4°C due to thermal expansion. Therefore, the understanding of water's density at various temperatures is crucial for many scientific and engineering applications.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Mirage is an illustration of
options: only total internal reflection of light.
both refraction and total internal reflection of light.
questions.solution:
A mirage is an illustration of both refraction and total internal reflection of light.

Explanation:

Mirages occur when layers of air at different temperatures cause light to bend. This is primarily due to refraction, which is the bending of light as it passes from one medium to another with a different density.

In the case of a hot road, the air near the surface is significantly warmer than the air above it. Light from the sky bends as it moves through these layers of varying density, and in some cases, this bending can continue until the angle of incidence exceeds the critical angle for total internal reflection.

Key Concepts:

Refraction: Occurs when light waves pass from one medium to another and change speed, causing the light to bend. This bending creates the initial path alteration of light.

Total Internal Reflection: After sufficient bending, if light strikes the boundary at a specific angle (greater than the critical angle), it reflects entirely back into the medium instead of passing through.

Together, refraction and total internal reflection help explain why a mirage makes a distant object, like the sky, appear as though it is reflecting off the ground, creating the illusion of water. Hence, Option D - both refraction and total internal reflection of light - is correct.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText:
Which of the following statements give characteristics of contact forces?

1. It appears between an object when it is in contact with some other object

2. It satisfies the third law of motion

3. It may appear between a pair of solid and fluid

Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
1. Definition of contact force

    – A force that arises only when two bodies are in physical contact.

  2. Check statement (1): “It appears between an object when it is in contact with some other object”

    – This is exactly the definition of a contact force.

    ⇒ Statement 1 is correct.

  3. Check statement (2): “It satisfies the third law of motion”

    – Newton’s third law, “To every action there is an equal and opposite reaction,” holds for all forces, including contact forces.

    ⇒ Statement 2 is correct.

  4. Check statement (3): “It may appear between a pair of solid and fluid”

    – Examples:

    • Fluid pressure on a submerged plate (normal contact force)

    • Viscous drag between a fluid and a moving solid

    ⇒ Statement 3 is correct.

All three statements are true.

Answer: Option 4 (1, 2 and 3)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements with respect to Global Positioning System (GPS) is not correct?
options: It is based on network of satellites orbiting above the Earth.
It is based on the system of triangulation.
GPS receivers provide location in terms of latitude, longitude and altitude.
It provides information exclusively for military operations.
questions.solution:
The statement with respect to Global Positioning System (GPS) that is not correct is:

Option D: It provides information exclusively for military operations.

Explanation:

Option A: It is based on a network of satellites orbiting above the Earth.

This statement is correct. GPS relies on a constellation of satellites that orbit the Earth and transmit signals. GPS receivers use these signals to determine their position.

Option B: It is based on the system of triangulation.

This statement is slightly misleading in its wording. While GPS positioning is often referred to using the concept of triangulation, it actually uses a method called trilateration. Trilateration involves measuring distances from multiple satellites (not angles which is more typical for triangulation) to pinpoint a location.

Option C: GPS receivers provide location in terms of latitude, longitude, and altitude.

This statement is correct. GPS receivers can precisely calculate a location's latitude, longitude, and altitude (elevation) using signals from multiple satellites.

Option D: It provides information exclusively for military operations.

This statement is incorrect. While GPS was originally developed by the U.S. Department of Defense and was initially intended for military purposes, it is now widely used for civilian applications as well. GPS technology is utilized in various fields including navigation, agriculture, surveying, time synchronization, and more.

To summarize, the correct answer is Option D.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Lightning is due to
options: The flow of charges between different parts of the cloud
The short-circuiting of charges between the upper and lower surfaces of the cloud
The collection of positively charged particles on the base and collection of negatively charged particles at the top of the cloud
The induction of positive charge on the ground below the negative charge at the base of the cloud
questions.solution:
Option 1

  1. Kondensation and collision of water‐droplets and ice particles in a cumulonimbus cloud → frictional charging.

  2. Lighter ice crystals (positive) carried to the top, heavier water/ice (negative) settle at the base → large separation of charge.

  3. Potential difference between different regions of the cloud (or between cloud and ground) increases.

  4. When electric field exceeds air’s dielectric strength, a sudden discharge (spark) occurs – this is lightning.

  5. Thus lightning is essentially the rapid flow of charges between oppositely charged parts of the cloud.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Ms. Rani decides to convert her AC generator into a DC generator. Which one of the following she would need to use?
questions.solution:
To convert an AC generator into a DC generator, Ms. Rani would need to use a split-ring type commutator.

Here’s a detailed explanation for understanding the reason behind this choice:

An AC generator, also known as an alternator, produces alternating current (AC), where the current periodically reverses direction. The critical component that reverses the direction of current in an AC generator is the slip rings and brushes.

On the other hand, a DC generator produces direct current (DC), where the current flows in one single direction. To achieve this, the generator requires a component that can ensure unidirectional flow of current. This component is known as the split-ring type commutator. The split-ring commutator serves to reverse the connection of the coil to the external circuit at the exact moment when the direction of induced current reverses, hence converting the AC to DC.

Here’s how the options fare in this context:

Option A: A split-ring type commutator

The split-ring type commutator is exactly what is needed to convert the alternating current produced by the generator into direct current. This option is correct.

Option B: Slip rings and brushes

Slip rings are used in AC generators to provide a continuous connection between the rotating coil and the external circuit, allowing for the flow of AC. This option is incorrect as they are used for AC, not DC.

Option C: A stronger magnetic field

While a stronger magnetic field could affect the magnitude of the induced EMF (Electromotive Force), it does not convert AC to DC. Hence, this option is incorrect.

Option D: A rectangular wire loop

A rectangular wire loop is a component of the coil within the generator, but it does not affect the type of current (AC or DC) being produced. Therefore, this option is also incorrect.

Thus, the correct answer is Option A: A split-ring type commutator.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: For a material under static conditions, which one among the following statements is correct ?
options: Both perfect conductor and insulator can be charged
Both perfect conductor and insulator cannot be charged
questions.solution:
Static conditions mean that electric charges are not moving. They stay still.

In an insulator, charges cannot move freely. They stay in one place where they are put.

This means an insulator can keep an electric charge when conditions are static.

In a conductor, charges can move around easily. This makes it hard to keep the charges in one spot.

⇒ Under static conditions, an insulator can be charged.

Therefore, the correct answer is Option 1.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following findings is NOT observed in Rutherford's Nuclear Model of Atom?
options: There is a neutral centre in an atom called nucleus
Nearly all the mass of an atom resides in the nucleus
The electrons revolve around the nucleus in a circular path
The size of a nucleus is very small as compared to the size of atom
questions.solution:
The statement NOT supported by Rutherford’s model is:

Option 1: “There is a neutral centre in an atom called nucleus.”

Rutherford’s gold-foil experiment showed the nucleus is extremely small, contains nearly all the atom’s mass and carries a positive charge. Electrons do revolve around it in the surrounding empty space.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: In which one among the following situations, the bulb ⊗ would glow the most ? (Consider all batteries are the same)
questions.solution:
CONCEPT:

Power in a Bulb and Types of Circuits

Power, which makes a bulb glow, is found with the formula:

P = V2 / R

Here, P is power, V is voltage, and R is resistance.

In a series circuit, the battery's voltage is split between all the parts. In a parallel circuit, each part gets the full battery voltage.

EXPLANATION:

The bulb shines the brightest when it gets as much voltage as possible because more voltage means more power (according to the formula above).

If the bulb is in a parallel circuit, it will get the full battery voltage. This makes it glow more.

If the bulb is in a series circuit with other things, the voltage is shared, so the bulb gets less voltage and glows less.

So, the bulb will glow the most if it is connected in parallel.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: If the current through an electrical machine running on direct current is $15 A$ and the machine runs for $10$ minutes, the charge that passes through the machine during this time is:
questions.solution:
To determine the charge that passes through the machine, we use the relationship between current, charge, and time. The formula that relates these quantities is given by :

$$ Q = I \times t $$

where :

$$ Q $$ is the charge (in coulombs, C)

$$ I $$ is the current (in amperes, A)

$$ t $$ is the time (in seconds, s)

We are given :

Current, $$ I = 15 \, \text{A} $$

Time, $$ t = 10 \, \text{minutes} $$

First, we need to convert the time from minutes to seconds :

$$ 10 \, \text{minutes} \times 60 \, \text{seconds per minute} = 600 \, \text{seconds} $$

Now, substituting the given values into the formula, we get :

$$ Q = 15 \, \text{A} \times 600 \, \text{s} $$

$$ Q = 9000 \, \text{C} $$

Therefore, the charge that passes through the machine during this time is :

Option D : $$ 9000 \, \text{C} $$
